prompt:
Platform team ticket. Our CI promotion gate is a pile of `kubectl get job -w | grep` shell glue and it misses failures whenever the apiserver rolls and the watch dies. I want a small dependable Go package, `jobwatch`, that speaks the real Kubernetes list+watch protocol itself — stdlib only, no client-go, no third-party modules (the module is already set up as `go-k8s-job-watch`).

Research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to confirm the CURRENT official Kubernetes documentation on kubernetes.io — the API Concepts page (watch streams, bookmarks, resourceVersion semantics, 410 recovery, the Status kind) and the batch/v1 Job reference (paths, JobStatus conditions). Our pinned understanding of the wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live docs against them — if they disagree, call that out explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the package must export (package jobwatch at the module root; split files however you like — client, watcher, decoding — I'd expect 3+ source files):

- NewClient(baseURL, token string, hc *http.Client) *Client — bearer token on every request, JSON accept header. ListJobs(ctx, namespace) returning a JobList{Items []Job; ResourceVersion string} taken from the list metadata. Job is a flat struct: Name, Namespace, UID, ResourceVersion, Active, Succeeded, Failed, Conditions []Condition{Type, Status, Reason, Message}, plus a TerminalCondition() (string, bool) helper — read the current docs for which condition types actually mean a Job is finished (hint: it is NOT every condition with status True). API errors decode the standard error body into *APIError{Code, Reason, Message}.
- NewWatcher(c *Client, namespace string) with Run(ctx, sink func(Event)) error. Run lists first (no resourceVersion pinned), reports the initial state through the sink, then watches from the list's version with bookmarks enabled. Watch events stream in as newline-delimited frames and a frame can be split across reads, so decode the stream properly. Bookmarks advance the resume version but never reach the sink. If the server ends the stream cleanly, re-watch from where you left off. When the version you asked for has expired, the server tells you in one of two ways — handle BOTH — and recovery is a fresh list diffed against what you already know (by UID) so downstream consumers see exactly what changed: docs/contract.json pins the exact diff-and-ordering rules we agreed on with the platform SIG folks; read it. Cancelling the context must make Run return ctx.Err() promptly, closing the connection. Any other API error surfaces as *APIError and stops the watcher.
- WaitForJob(ctx, c, namespace, name) (Job, error) — run a watcher until that Job reaches a terminal condition and return the terminal Job. A failed Job is a result, not an error.

Acceptance is jobwatch_test.go — it spins up a loopback fake apiserver, so no cluster and no real credentials are involved. Run: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — until it's green. jobwatch_test.go and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: Kubernetes API server (batch/v1 Job, list/watch protocol)
research.interface: Kubernetes REST API batch/v1 (stable), raw list+watch protocol as documented for API clients; researched against v1.36

official_sources.title: Kubernetes API Concepts
official_sources.used_for: watch=true streaming of newline-delimited WatchEvent frames (ADDED/MODIFIED/DELETED/BOOKMARK/ERROR), allowWatchBookmarks, resourceVersion semantics for list and watch, 410 Gone / reason Expired recovery by relisting, and the meta/v1 Status error kind

official_sources.title: Job | Kubernetes (kubernetes-api reference)
official_sources.used_for: batch/v1 GET /apis/batch/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/jobs list and watch paths, JobList shape, JobStatus.active/succeeded/failed counters, and JobCondition semantics (Complete/Failed/Suspended with status True)

official_sources.title: Kubernetes v1.36 official OpenAPI specification (kubernetes/kubernetes, release-1.36)
official_sources.used_for: exact query parameters of the namespaced Job list/watch operation (watch, allowWatchBookmarks, resourceVersion, resourceVersionMatch), the WatchEvent {type,object} definition, JobCondition fields, and Status fields (status/message/reason/code)

research.language_rationale: client-go is the official Go SDK, but this integration deliberately targets the documented raw list/watch HTTP protocol: kubernetes.io 'API Concepts' specifies it as the supported contract for API clients, and it is exactly what client-go's reflector implements underneath. Go's stdlib net/http plus a streaming encoding/json decoder is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to implement it, and the harness forbids third-party modules.
